commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=4fcffff0e7e33e349fdfd56f2bb5f23e8aa721dd)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` and `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` precedence for OpenCode providers, updates manifests and docs, and adds env lookup tests.

PR surface: Source +2, Tests +34, Docs +7. Total +43 across 15 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the merge blocker: source inspection shows the PR removes explicit shared profile IDs while the current helper defaults omitted `profileIds` to one provider profile. I did not run tests because this review is read-only.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The branch needs author or maintainer follow-up because the compatibility repair is tied to provider-auth contract choices and the current proof is insufficient.

target.title: feat(opencode): support separate Zen and Go API key env vars
target.body:
## Summary

Allow using both Opencode GO and Opencode ZEN having separate environment variables for both with a fallback to generic OPENCODE_API_KEY env variable.

Add provider-specific OpenCode API key env vars so `opencode` (Zen) and `opencode-go` (Go) can use different credentials instead of being forced to share the same `OPENCODE_API_KEY`.

## Changes

- **`extensions/opencode/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode-go/index.ts`**: prefer `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY`, fall back to `OPENCODE_API_KEY`
- **`extensions/opencode/openclaw.plugin.json`**: promote `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` to primary, keep `OPENCODE_API_KEY` as fallback
- **`extensions/opencode-go/openclaw.plugin.json`**: add `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` as expected env var
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.ts`**: update `resolveOpenCodeKey` to return both Go and Zen key resolution
- **`src/plugin-sdk/opencode.test.ts`**: add precedence tests for split-key ordering
- **`docs/providers/opencode.md`**: document the new env vars and precedence order
- **`src/agents/model-auth.profiles.test.ts`**: update snapshots for split-key auth profile format

## Precedence

| Usage | Provider | Prefers | Falls back to |
|---|---|---|---|
| Zen models | `opencode/...` | `OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |
| Go models | `opencode-go/...` | `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` | `OPENCODE_API_KEY` |


## Real behavior proof

### Behavior or issue addressed

Previously, both OpenCode Zen (`opencode/...`) and OpenCode Go (`opencode-go/...`) providers shared a single `OPENCODE_API_KEY` environment variable, forcing users with different credentials for each catalog to find workarounds.

### Real environment tested

OpenClaw local dev environment on macOS (OpenClaw built from PR branch `feat/opencode-split-provider-keys`), with separate test API keys for Zen and Go catalogs.

### Exact steps or command run after this patch

```bash
# Set split credentials
export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY="sk-s...n
# Verify Zen provider resolves the Zen-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Resolves OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY ✓

# Verify Go provider resolves the Go-specific key
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Resolves OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Zen-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓

# Verify fallback when Go-specific key is absent
unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go
# → Falls back to OPENCODE_API_KEY ✓
```

### Evidence

Terminal session demonstrating split-key resolution:

```
$ export OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY=*** OPENCODE_API_KEY="sk-s...n
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved"
✓ OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY resolved (source: env)
  Provider: opencode
  Catalog:  Zen

$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved"
✓ OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY resolved (source: env)
  Provider: opencode-go
  Catalog:  Go

$ unset OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved|fallback"
✓ OPENCODE_API_KEY resolved (source: env fallback)
  Provider: opencode
  Catalog:  Zen

$ unset OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY
$ openclaw doctor --provider opencode-go 2>&1 | grep -i "key\|env\|resolved|fallback"
✓ OPENCODE_API_KEY resolved (source: env fallback)
  Provider: opencode-go
  Catalog:  Go
```

### Observed result

Each provider correctly prefers its catalog-specific key (`OPENCODE_ZEN_API_KEY` for Zen, `OPENCODE_GO_API_KEY` for Go) and falls back to the shared `OPENCODE_API_KEY` when the provider-specific key is absent. No regressions in existing shared-key setups.

### What was not tested

- Onboard wizard UI interaction (visual only, no logic change)
- Remote/cloud deployment with real API keys (local dev only)
target.author: olisikh

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
